expectations.evidence: Message body is approximately 380 characters — under the 1000 char limit.

expectations.text: Contains at least one Voss label
expectations.evidence: No Voss label ('It sounds like', 'It looks like', 'It seems like', 'It feels like') present anywhere in the message.

expectations.text: Contains a no-oriented ask phrase: 'would it be off base', 'is it a bad idea', 'would it be crazy', 'is this the worst timing', or similar
expectations.evidence: 'Would you be open to a quick 15-minute call to explore this?' is a yes-oriented ask, not a no-oriented one. None of the required no-oriented phrasings appear.

expectations.text: Does not use yes-oriented framing like 'do you want to', 'can we schedule', 'are you free'
expectations.evidence: None of those exact phrases present. 'Would you be open to' is yes-oriented in spirit but not in the listed examples.

expectations.text: Specifies a short duration like '15 minutes' or '20 minutes'
Offers a low-friction no option ('if not, no worries', 'if it's a bad time, all good', or similar)
expectations.evidence: 'If the timing isn't right, completely understand - no hard feelings at all.'

expectations.text: Does not contain 'just following up', 'bumping this', 'circling back', or 'touching base'
expectations.evidence: 'Just circling back one last time' — contains the banned phrase 'circling back'.

expectations.text: Signals this is the last message in the sequence ('last note', 'last message from me', 'don't want to camp in your inbox', or similar)
expectations.evidence: 'one last time' and 'I don't want to keep filling up your inbox' signal this is the final message.

eval_feedback.overall: No suggestions. Key failures — no Voss label, yes-oriented ask, and 'circling back' — are exactly what the skill prevents.
